title: My School
focus: Places in the building, school staff and their jobs, supplies, reading my schedule.
function: Locate places and people in my school and say where I need to go
grammar: I need to go to the ___.
This is Ms./Mr. ___. She/He is the ___.
foundationalLiteracy: Letter–sound correspondence for initial consonants using school words. Reading numerals on doors and matching them to a written schedule.
culturalNote: School structures differ enormously. Passing periods, lockers, bells, and choosing your own lunch may all be new. Some students come from schools where you stay in one room all day, and some have never been in a school building at all. Walk the building physically before teaching the words.

1.canDo: Point to a place in the school when it is named aloud.
1.scaffold: Picture map of the building with icons. Teacher names one place; student points.
1.check: Points to the correct place for 5 of 6 named locations.
1.l1Bridge: Building photos labeled in the home language on first exposure only.

2.canDo: Understand a short spoken direction to a place in the building.
2.scaffold: Simplified floor map with a start marker. Direction repeated on request.
2.check: Traces the correct route on the map from a 2-step spoken direction.
2.l1Bridge: Direction words pre-taught in the home language before the map task.

3.canDo: Follow a multi-step announcement and identify where I need to go and when.
3.scaffold: Note-catcher with where / when / who columns. Audio at normal rate, played twice.
3.check: Extracts place, time, and person from a 4-sentence announcement.
3.l1Bridge: Note-catcher headings glossed; the announcement stays in English.

1.canDo: Name a place in the school and say it when I need to go there.
1.scaffold: Bathroom/nurse pass cards with the word and icon. One-word response accepted.
1.check: Says or shows the correct place word to request permission.
1.l1Bridge: Student may name the place in the home language while showing the pass card; teacher supplies the English.

2.canDo: Ask where a place is and say where I need to go.
2.scaffold: Frame strip: Where is the ___? / I need to go to the ___.
2.check: Asks a staff member for a location using a full framed question.
2.l1Bridge: Rehearse 'Where is the ___?' after hearing the equivalent question in the home language.

3.canDo: Give a classmate directions to a place in the building.
3.scaffold: Preposition bank (next to, across from, upstairs) plus a map to reference.
3.check: Produces a 2–3 step direction a peer can actually follow.
3.l1Bridge: Plan the route in the home language, give the directions in English.

1.canDo: Recognize school signs and room numbers.
1.scaffold: Real photographs of the building's own signage. Word–photo matching.
1.check: Matches 5 signs (office, exit, restroom, library, nurse) to photos.
1.l1Bridge: Pair each real sign photo with its home-language meaning once, then remove the gloss.

2.canDo: Read my class schedule and find the room for a given period.
2.scaffold: Enlarged schedule with color-coded periods and an icon per subject.
2.check: Names the correct room and teacher for 3 requested periods.
2.l1Bridge: Schedule column headers (period, room, teacher) glossed in the home language.

3.canDo: Read a short staff directory and say who to go to for a problem.
3.scaffold: Directory with roles glossed in plain language. Three scenario cards.
3.check: Matches 3 scenarios (sick, lost schedule, new to school) to the right staff member.
3.l1Bridge: Staff role names glossed — job titles rarely transfer directly between school systems.

1.canDo: Copy my room numbers and teacher names onto a schedule card.
1.scaffold: Pre-printed grid; student copies from the official schedule into a pocket card.
1.check: Produces a legible personal schedule card with correct room numbers.
1.l1Bridge: Room numbers are language-neutral; start here to build early success.

2.canDo: Label a map of my school with the places I use.
2.scaffold: Blank building map, word bank of 8 places, icons on the bank.
2.l1Bridge: Map word bank shown bilingually; labels written in English.

3.canDo: Write a short note asking to go somewhere or asking for help.
3.scaffold: Model note plus a polite-phrase bank (May I…, I need…, Thank you).
3.check: Writes a 2-sentence note with a request and a courtesy phrase.
3.l1Bridge: Draft the note in the home language if needed, then write the English version.

1.scaffold: Points to a picture card and says the single place word with rising intonation. Pass card in hand. Teacher models the full question back.

2.scaffold: Frame strip taped inside the notebook cover. Practices with a partner before asking real staff.

3.say: Excuse me, I'm looking for the [place]. I have [subject] next period. Is it [direction]?
3.scaffold: Open frame plus a preposition bank. Confirms understanding by repeating the direction back.
